content.verdict: Saffire is the all-inclusive 20-suite cliffside lodge above Coles Bay, with a sweeping curve of glass framing the Hazards mountain range and Wineglass Bay below. Architecturally one of the most awarded lodges in the world (RAIA's Sir Zelman Cowen Award). All-inclusive: meals, drinks, premium activities (oyster farm, abalone dive, Tasmanian devil close encounter, Wineglass Bay hike). Romantic in a quietly Tasmanian way — no glitz, just exceptional landscape and impeccable execution.
content.best_room: Private Pavilion (1 unit, $3,500+/night) is the honeymoon dream — ocean view, secluded position. Deluxe Suites (Hazards-facing) have the iconic mountain view. Signature Suites (slightly smaller, more affordable) keep the Wineglass Bay angle. All have heated bathroom floors and Aesop products.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival from Hobart or Launceston
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Hobart (HBA) or Launceston (LST). 2.5h scenic drive via the East Coast (vineyards, oyster farms). Welcome on Saffire's central glass-curve terrace with sparkling Tasmanian wine. Settle into your suite. Dinner at Palate restaurant — Cape Grim beef, Bruny Island cheese.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wineglass Bay hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning hike to Wineglass Bay Lookout (1h up, 1h descent to the beach itself, 4h round-trip if you go to the beach). One of Australia's most photographed beaches. Picnic packed by the kitchen. Return for in-suite massage. Sunset over the Hazards from your deck.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Marine Farm experience
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk into the Saffire-leased oyster farm (in waders, in the water). Shuck and eat oysters straight from the ocean with a glass of Bay of Fires Riesling. Most-loved single experience the lodge offers. Lunch at the lodge. Afternoon spa Cinq Mondes ritual.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tasmanian devil encounter + Bay of Fires
itinerary_7_nights.description: Behind-the-scenes visit to a Tasmanian devil sanctuary (lodge-arranged, hands-off, conservation-focused). Lunch at the Devil's Corner Cellar Door (sweeping vineyard view). Continue to the Bay of Fires for orange-lichen-rock sunset photos. Late dinner at Palate.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sailing on Coles Bay
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day private skippered yacht in Coles Bay — pristine water, dolphins, wedge-tailed eagles overhead. Lunch on board with Tasmanian seafood platter. Return mid-afternoon. Sundown cocktails at the Saffire bar overlooking the Hazards.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa day + abalone dive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Optional abalone dive (lodge-arranged, qualified divers only) or stay at the lodge for a full spa day — couples' Tasmanian seaweed cocoon, hot stone massage. Final dinner at Palate with the chef's tasting menu paired with Tasmanian Pinot Noir.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast & East Coast drive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast on your private deck — local berry compote, smoked Tasmanian salmon, fresh granola. Drive back to Launceston via Tasmania's Whisky Trail (Lark, Sullivans Cove tasting). Evening departure flight.

caveats: All-inclusive but premium activities (helicopter, abalone dive) are still extras.
Tasmania's weather is unpredictable — pack layers including a rain shell year-round.
20 suites total — books out 6+ months for Dec–March (Tasmanian summer).
Drive from Hobart (2.5h) or Launceston (2h) — there is no closer airport.
Restaurant dinner runs 7–9pm only; no late-night dining option.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Outstanding — small scale, all-inclusive, dramatic landscape, and Australia's most awarded lodge architecture. The oyster-farm experience and Wineglass Bay hike together create a defining Tasmanian honeymoon.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December–March (Tasmanian summer, 18–22°C, long evenings). October/November for fewer crowds. Winter is dramatic and quieter — fireplaces and red wine season.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 6+ months for the Private Pavilion or Hazards-view Deluxe Suites in peak summer.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No formal restriction, but children under 12 are rare and the lodge atmosphere is firmly adult-leaning.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Private Pavilion if budget allows; otherwise a Deluxe Suite for the Hazards mountain view.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Hobart (HBA) or Launceston (LST). 2-2.5h scenic drive to Coles Bay. Saffire arranges private transfer ($600–$800 per car each way).
